The hot pattern is equivalent to:
F.pad(torch.cat([cache_x, x], dim=2), (Wl, Wr, Ht, Hb, Dl - cache_t, Dr))
fo

Motivation
Jdopensource/JoyAI-Echo (JoyEcho) is a long-form audio–video generation model built on the LTX-2 backbone. Its core idea is a paired audio–video memory bank: each shot commits decoded frames and audio latents into a rolling bank, and subsequent shots condition on that memory prefix — enab

Motivation
Follow-up to #27720. That PR was intended to swap the dual-stream execution order so the routed experts run on the main stream (with the deferred MoE finalize fused into the main-stream residual add), but only the deferred-finalize half landed — forward_normal_dual_stream was left with sh

Result: for a per-quant image override, the Playground

sglang.title: [diffusion][perf]: Shard Qwen Text Embed in SP
sglang.author: avjves
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

Currently Qwen Image (and alternatives) don't shard text embedding in SP and replicate it on all GPUs (Similarly to FLUX before #27066). This saves some comms, but balloons the GEMMs.
This PR shards the text embedding when it's divisable by the number of GPUs. This does not happen always

sglang.title: [Diffusion] Fuse LTX2 Ada values
sglang.author: BBuf
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Fuse the LTX-2.3 Ada value materialization path used by LTX2TransformerBlock.
The block repeatedly computes:
scale_shift_table[None, None] + timestep.reshape(batch, -1, total_params, hidden)
and then slices out the self-attention, MLP, and prompt-cross-attention Ada values. For the cross_

sglang.title: Fix MiniMax MSA fallback when fmha plan is unavailable
sglang.author: BBuf

MiniMax-M3 can route the main sparse-attention step through the optional MSA fmha_sm100 backend on Blackwell. The availability gate only checked that fmha_sm100 could be imported, but the actual fast path also requires the fmha_sm100_plan API.
On images where the module is present but the

RuntimeError: The size of tensor a (90006) must match the size of tensor b (90007) at non-singleton dimension

Replace the check-then-bind pattern (get_open_port + socket.bind) in the SHM broadcaster with ZMQ's atomic bind_to_random_port, eliminating a TOCTOU race that caused EADDRINUSE under dp-attention with attn_tp==2.
When SGLANG_PORT is set, the search range is [SGLANG_PORT, SGLANG_PORT+8]; oth

sglang.title: Fix IPv6 wildcard bootstrap address resolution in disagg

sglang.bodyExcerpt:

When the server is bound to a wildcard address (0.0.0.0 or ::), the register_to_bootstrap PUT now targets the same-family loopback (127.0.0.1 / ::1) instead of self.local_ip, which can resolve to a different address family than the listener.
Updated tests to verify loopback behavior.

This batch extends the effort from test/regist

sglang.title: [HiCache] remove large host mem constraint
sglang.author: xiezhq-hermann
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
This PR removes the hardcoded constraint that requires host memory pool to be larger than the device pool, including changes and guards in cache write policy implementations. This will serve as a stepping stone for a more comprehensive buffer-only mode implementation as explored in other

sglang.title: fix(spec): track current_token in ReasonerGrammarObject
sglang.author: IvanShan177
sglang.bodyExcerpt: ReasonerGrammarObject.accept_token forwarded the token only to the inner grammar and never set its own current_token. Disaggregation's process_prebuilt uses req.grammar.current_token is None to detect a retracted/re-prebuilt request whose last token was already accepted and must not be re-accepted.

sglang.title: Skip FlashInfer FP8 autotune for MXFP8 quantized models
sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

MXFP8 (microscaling) models dispatch to flashinfer's mm_mxfp8, which the FP8 autotune dummy run triggers an illegal memory access on.
The mxfp8 gemm is fixed-config and needs no tuning, so skip autotune when quantization contains "mxfp8".

Original commits

8ec074d92



CI States
Latest PR

sglang.author: hnyls2002

Adds a global edge middleware that counts every request at the router's HTTP boundary:

sgl_router_requests_total{route, method} — incremented at entry (before worker pick), so it counts true intake including requests later parked, shed, or cancelled before dispatch.
sgl_router_responses_total{route

sglang.title: [KDA-Pilot] Add diffusion residual-gate CUDA fast path for LTX2
sglang.author: BBuf

Motivation
Add a native CUDA JIT fast path for the LTX-2.3 residual-gate update pattern from KDA-Pilot task b200_diffusion_residual_gate_add__multi_shape / BBuf/KDA-Pilot#134.
The hot pattern is equivalent to:
hidden_states = hidden_states + update * gate
for contiguous [B, L, D] tensors with either

sglang.title: [DSA] Make seq_lens_cpu optional for DeepSeek Sparse Attention
sglang.author: kpham-sgl
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Opts DSA out of the host D2H sync now that all spec-v2 paths replay under CUDA graphs.

needs_cpu_seq_lens = False on both DSA backends → resolve_seq_lens_cpu skips the fwd_prepare_d2h_stream.synchronize() on the decode critical path.
Eager init_forward_metadata tolerates seq_lens_cpu=None (derives

sglang.title: [DSA] Remove host H2D sync in _apply_cuda_graph_metadata
sglang.author: kpham-sgl
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
_apply_cuda_graph_metadata built the per-req QO-length tensor with torch.tensor([num_draft_tokens] * bs, device=cuda) — a pageable H2D copy that triggers a cudaStreamSynchronize (~8.5 ms/step on GB200) during graph replay.
Use torch.full((bs,), num_draft_tokens, ...) instead (async, on-device, ident

sglang.title: [server_args] Make MoRI per-rank dispatch-token requirement overridable
sglang.author: zx3xyy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
The MoRI chunked-prefill check asserts the dispatch buffer
(SGLANG_MORI_NUM_MAX_DISPATCH_TOKENS_PER_RANK) covers a full
chunked_prefill_size. Setups that shard the sequence across ranks
(sequence parallelism) only dispatch a per-rank shard through MoRI -- fewer
than chunked_prefill_size toke

sglang.title: [mori] Add a combine-kwargs hook and use_external_inp_buf plumbing
sglang.author: zx3xyy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
Make the MoRI EP combine path extensible:

Add a _combine_kwargs(hidden_states) hook on _MoriEPDispatcherImplBase
(default returns {}), passed through to mori_op.combine / combine_send,
so subclasses can supply extra combine arguments (e.g. an external output
buffer for a zero-copy combine).

back to a per-elemen

Perf optimization
MiniMax-M3 runs its single shared expert as a separate dense MLP every MoE layer

into the routed grouped GEMM removes those per-layer launches, which is the dominant cost
at low/medium concur

macOS CPU builds never passed -fopenmp (regression from #16086, which added omp.h but not the flag), so the kernels' #pragma omp parallel regions were compiled out and ran serially while omp_get_max_threads() still reported all cores. The attention split-KV path then spins on an in-region ba

Specifically:

Adds @ivanium to existing KV-related entries: kv_transfer, config/cache.py, v1/core,

vllm.title: [ROCm][Bugfix] Pass num_kv_splits to aiter mla_reduce_v1
vllm.author: Rohan138
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
#46692 bumped AITER to v0.1.16.post2, which includes ROCm/aiter#3391. That aiter PR added a required num_kv_splits argument to mla_reduce_v1 (now position 7 of the registered op schema). The FP8 ASM prefill reduce call in the AITER dense MLA backend (rocm_aiter_mla.py) was not updated, so on

vllm.title: [CI] Raise gsm8k startup timeout for Qwen3 NVFP4 trtllm configs
vllm.author: khluu
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Two B200 gsm8k evals fail during server startup, not on accuracy, in builds such as #74616:

LM Eval Small Models (B200) → test_gsm8k_correctness[Qwen3-30B-A3B-NVFP4]
LM Eval Large Models (B200, EP) → test_gsm8k_correctness[Qwen3-Next-80B-A3B-NVFP4-EP2]

Both die with RuntimeError: Server fa

vllm.title: Fix Transformers backend FP8 MoE and remove some boilerplate
vllm.author: hmellor
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The Transformers backend was eagerly storing expert_weights before quantization. This meant that when using FP8 models a stale reference to the unquantized expert weights was kept and the memory usage is higher than expected.
This PR fixes the issue by removing the eager population of self.expert_we

vllm.title: [ModelRunner V2] Deduplicate ModelState init logic
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Just simplification, no functional changes.

vllm.title: [CI] Raise gsm8k startup timeout for MoE Refactor Qwen3 NVFP4 configs
vllm.author: khluu
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Follow-up to #46881. The MoE Refactor Integration B200 jobs hit the same FlashInfer NVFP4 startup-timeout as the LM Eval jobs fixed in #46881. In builds such as #74616, these configs fail with RuntimeError: Server failed to start in time. — the model loads fine, but FlashInfer FP4 kernel aut

vllm.title: [ModelRunner V2] Fix cross-attention block table sizing
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Without this there can be OOB errors with e.g. Nemotron-Parse

vllm.title: [CI] Don't try and download files that we already know don't exist
vllm.author: hmellor
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
huggingface_hub keeps track of files which were requested but do not exist. This information persists in the HF cache. It does this so that if you request this file again, huggingface_hub doesn't have to make a request because it already knows this file doesn't exist.
The documentation for this is h

vllm.title: Vram semaphore infra
vllm.author: brandonpelfrey
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Continuing the work described in RFC #30839, this change introduces two new things:

A new video decoding backend based on pynvvideocodec which is a lightweight dependency enabling HW video decoding on all NVIDIA GPUs. Video decoding always takes place using GPU index 0.
A VRAM Semaphore. Th

vllm.title: [ModelRunner V2] Support realtime embeddings
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Realtime models like voxtral require embeddings for decode steps too.

vllm.title: [Build] Update vllm to point to vllm-project/flash-attention commit that builds FA3 with torch stable API.
vllm.author: cleonard530
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
As a part of #26946. Updated vllm_flash_attn.cmake to use FA fork that builds a libtorch ABI stable FA3 library.
Currently, this is pointing towards my flash-attention fork but if this passes the CI, we will merge that fork with vllm-project/flash-attention, then we will point to the new tag

vllm.title: [Build] Show error message when using ROCm with LTO and different compilers
vllm.author: davispuh
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Currently when default compiler /usr/bin/c++ is GCC and when building with LTO (-flto=auto) when ROCm is using Clang then created shared libraries _C.abi3.so _moe_C.abi3.so, _rocm_C.abi3.so will be missing symbols like PyInit__C.
$ vllm serve
WARNING 02-24 16:24:26 [rocm.py:38] Failed to imp

vllm.title: fix(docker): eliminate race conditions in shared buildkit cache mounts
vllm.author: weizhoublue
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Root Cause:
Omission of  sharing=locked  on  --mount=type=cache  defaults BuildKit to  sharing=shared .
Multiple containers read/write same host directory concurrently, causing cargo write collisions.
Remedy:
Add  sharing=locked  to cargo cache mounts in Dockerfile , matching configuration i

vllm.title: [ROCm][CI] Relax fused layernorm quant test tolerances for one-ULP outliers
vllm.author: divakar-amd
vllm.bodyExcerpt: The fused RMSNorm+quant kernels compute the normalized intermediate and quantization in one pass, while the reference path runs a native fp32 RMS norm followed by a separate quant kernel. The differing fp32 reduction order (and FMA contraction in the x * s_variance * w chain on HIP) occasionally fli

vllm.title: [ROCm][CI TG] refactor and fix deepep_moe test group
vllm.author: divakar-amd
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Fix test_deepep_moe.py on ROCm and consolidate MoE test accuracy checks


Buffer reuse (low-latency DeepEP): The modular kernel and its DeepEP all2all buffer are now built once and reused across all token chunks instead of being re-created per chunk. Re-creating it per chunk re-initializes rocSHMEM,

vllm.title: [MoE Backend] add HPC-Ops MoE backend
vllm.author: thisjiang
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Support hpc-ops moe backend. HPC-Ops is a production-grade, high-performance, and easy-to-use operator library for LLM inference, developed by the Tencent Hunyuan AI Infra team.
You can enable hpc-ops moe backend by specify --moe_backend hpc.
Test Plan
Unit test -- skip when hpc-ops not inst

vllm.title: [ROCm][CI]Fix test_concat_and_cache_mla_rope_fused on ROCm
vllm.author: divakar-amd
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This test was failing in CI on ROCm due to fp16 numerical mismatches between the reference and the fused kernel. The root cause is that the torch-native reference (forward_native) implicitly upcasts fp16×fp16 to fp32, while the fused CUDA kernel runs in native fp16.
(Also refer to the in-depth analy

vllm.title: fix: Correct reasoning-end detection for prompt history
vllm.author: jasonozuzu-cohere
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
This PR fixes a bug in the way our reasoning parser determines whether or not a collection of tokens is considered a concluded reasoning block via the is_reasoning_end method.
The original implementation simply scanned for the presence of any <|END_THINKING|> token, and could be called on th

vllm.title: [CLI] Add flag to print TTFT and TPS in `vllm chat`
vllm.author: benchislett
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
An opt-in mode to print the speed timings (TTFT and TPS, where TPS includes TTFT in the average, number of generated tokens and e2e latency) after each turn in vllm chat and vllm complete.
Opt-in to maintain current UX, but it's super handy for demos and quick sanity checks that correctness

vllm.title: [GLM5.2 Perf] `fused_indexer_q_rope_quant` triton kernel, 1.9% ~ 3.3% E2E Throughput improvement.
vllm.author: yewentao256
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
We have similar fused kernel in DSv4, this path works for GLM as wel
Originally:
Q RoPE -> cat -> FP8 quant -> q_scale fold into weights
Now:
Q RoPE + FP8 quant + q_scale fold into weights in one triton kernel.
Test
vllm serve zai-org/GLM-5.2-FP8   --kv-cache-dtype fp8_e4m3   --enable-expert

vllm.title: [ROCm][CI] Add TRITON_ATTN score absolute tolerance floor
vllm.author: peizhang56
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
The text_vs_text score (~0.10) in test_cross_encoder_online_vision.py drifts ~0.008 absolute on gfx942/ROCm 7.2 under the TRITON_ATTN backend. Because the value is near zero, this small fixed absolute drift reads as ~7.9% relative error and exceeds the tight 0.045 relative tolerance ~@~T eve
